# claude-managed-agents_environment (Data Source)

Look up an existing Claude Managed Agents environment by id.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing sandbox environment by id. Useful when the environment
# was created outside of Terraform (e.g. via the SDK) and you want to read
# its current networking policy or package list.

data "claude-managed-agents_environment" "existing" {
  id = "env_01HqR2k7vXbZ9mNpL3wYcT8f"
}

output "existing_env_name" {
  value = data.claude-managed-agents_environment.existing.name
}

output "existing_env_networking_type" {
  value = data.claude-managed-agents_environment.existing.config.networking.type
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Server-assigned identifier (e.g. `env_01ABC...`).

### Read-Only

- `archived_at` (String) ISO 8601 archive timestamp, or null if active.
- `config` (Attributes) Sandbox configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config))
- `created_at` (String) ISO 8601 creation timestamp.
- `name` (String) Environment name.
- `updated_at` (String) ISO 8601 last-modified timestamp.

<a id="nestedatt--config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config`

Read-Only:

- `networking` (Attributes) Outbound networking policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--networking))
- `packages` (Attributes) Per-package-manager install lists. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--packages))
- `type` (String) Config discriminator (currently always `cloud`).

<a id="nestedatt--config--networking"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.networking`

Read-Only:

- `allow_mcp_servers` (Boolean) Whether MCP-server traffic is allowed under `limited` networking.
- `allow_package_managers` (Boolean) Whether package-manager installs are allowed under `limited` networking.
- `allowed_hosts` (List of String) Hostnames the agent may reach.
- `type` (String) Either `unrestricted` or `limited`.


<a id="nestedatt--config--packages"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.packages`

Read-Only:

- `apt` (List of String) APT packages.
- `cargo` (List of String) Cargo crates.
- `gem` (List of String) RubyGems.
- `go` (List of String) Go modules.
- `npm` (List of String) npm packages.
- `pip` (List of String) pip packages.
